entry.title: IlegalArgumentException: Can not decode NULL or empty string.
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

Our Client has both an SRM application & Enterprise portal.

The Purpose of our SRM system is "To provide an e-tendering platform for managing the entire procurement of BMC across various departments. The platform is used for publishing and inviting responses for regular tenders as well as quotations. Further, EMD is accepted online."

 

On our Client website, the Encryption & Decryption mechanism is configured. A bid done by a vendor on the Client's Website. It will get encrypted So that no one will see the bid amount. After that, we log in to the SRM Enterprise Portal and click the Decrypt BID then the amount will get decrypted. But we are facing inconsistency here.

There are two bids, 1 bid is decrypted and able to view the amount but while decrypting the 2nd bid, it throws a below error. Also, attached the screenshots for your reference.

 

IlegalArgumentException: Can not decode NULL or empty string.
Further issue details: java. lang. IlegaArgumentException: Can not decode NULL or empty string.
at com.sap.india.srm.purchaserdecryptbid.PurchaserBase64Utilities.base64Decode(PurchaserBase64Utlities.java:54)
at com.sap.india.srm.purchaserdecryptbid.PurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.processAssemblingBrokenKeyPurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.java:307)
at com.sap.india.srm.purchaserdecryptbid.PurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.processDecryptAllBid(PurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.java:218)
at com.sap.india.sm.purchaserdecryptbid.PurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.init(PurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.java: 144)
at com.sap.india.srm.purchaserdecryptbid.PurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.startPurchaserOperationsPurchaserDecAssembleBrokenKey.java:76)
at com.sap.india.sm.main.MainApplet.start(MainApplet.java: 119)
at com.sun.deploy.uitoolkit.impl.awt.AWTAppletAdapter.start(Unknown Source)
at sun.plugin2.applet.Plugin2Manager$AppletExecutionRunnable.run(Unknown Source)
at java.lang. Thread.run Unknown Source)

 

Kindly help me out to resolve the issue.

 

Thanks in Advance!

Best Regards,

Azarudin

entry.title: Re: BAPI_FIXEDASSET_CREATE1 with equipment synchronization
entry.content:
Did you look for OSS notes relevant for your versions (notes such as 2999629 - Japan: CSMD - Time-dependent Fields Are Incorrect When You Execute BAPI_FIXEDASSET_CREATE1 Multiple Times )

if you don't find any suitable notes, you could try to execute the BAPI in another session

* Execute in a separate session
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_FIXEDASSET_CREATE1'
  DESTINATION 'NONE'
    " (...)
    TABLES
      return         = lt_return.
* Commit the changes within this session
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
  DESTINATION 'NONE'
    EXPORTING wait = 'X'.
* Close the session.
CALL FUNCTION 'RFC_CONNECTION_CLOSE'
  EXPORTING destination = 'NONE'
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 0.

So no data will be shared between successive calls due to explicit close.

entry.title: Crystal reports Displaying all parts of a product structure
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m new to Crystal Reports and I’m currently working on a project where I need to display all parts of our product structure in a report. I have successfully established a data link, but for some reason, the report only shows one of several parts of the product structure.

To give you a bit more context, I have a main product and each product has a structure (1-10 parts). However, only one of these parts is visible in the report. I’ve been wondering if this could be related to the fact that the data field of the structure uses and reads data like the data source of my main product. Could this be affecting the visibility of the other parts in the report? Should i be using subreport instead? if so, how?

I’ve checked my data links and they seem to be functioning correctly. I’m not sure why the other parts aren’t showing up. Any advice or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

 

SAP Crystal Reports

entry.title: Inbound Interface to receive the UUID, PDF and XML details through ODATA - MEXICO
entry.content:
Requirement

In Mexico, it is necessary that all companies receive tax authority validated XML and PDF files that contain the details of the payment made to vendors.

Its mandatory to extract the UUID number from the file that is received from the vendors and save it in a way that makes it easily accessible, in S4 it would be easier to store it in a custom table.

The importance of this interface is to store UUID in S4 system in order to facilitate users identify which invoices were payed to which vendors as well as reconciliation between EDICOM Portal and S4 system. The risk of not having this interface, is that files (PDF and XML) and UUID data will only be stored in EDICOM Portal and reconciliation to corresponding payments would be time consuming for users.   

Technical Requirement

Interaction of SAP with EDICOM portal where SAP CPI is the middleware which sends the XML, PDF to S4. The technical requirement was to create a ODATA which can receive XML and PDF, also should extract UUID details from XML structure and update it to the respective document.

Simple flow chart:

Detailed Flow chart:

                  

Technical Solution

To receive and extract the details from XML file, ODATA interface is developed and the received XML and PDF files are attached to the respective FI document.

XML and PDF files are sent in base64 encoded format from SAP CPI then in S4 we make use of various function modules to convert it and make the attachment.

Created ODATA service in tcode SEGW - ZFI_PAYMENT_COMPLEMENT_002

In Data model the custom table ZFI_IF020_PAYCOM is used to give ODATA the reference structure. The last two fields ZPDF & ZXML are created in RAWSTRING format to store the XML and PDF files.

Regenerate the Runtime Artifact ZCL_ZFI_PAYMENT_COM_01_DPC_EXT

This gives us the class where we can write the required logic, here we have redefined the method PAY_COMPSET_CREATE_ENTITY – holds the logic to validate the inputs received from SAP CPI and the logic to add PDF and XML to the FI document.

FI_DOCUMENT_CHANGE FM is used to update UUID to the respecting FI document.

The above FM is passed with the required importing parameters.

PDF Attachment:

PDF file is sent in Base64 format from CPI to S4.

  • SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY – using this FM the received base64 format is converted to binary
  • ARCHIV_CREATE_TABLE – using this FM the converted binary file is attached to the FI document

XML Attachment:

XML is also sent in Base64 format from CPI to S4.

  • SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY – using this FM the received base64 format is converted to binary
  • SO_CONVERT_CONTENTS_BIN – to get the binary file in tab format
  • SO_FOLDER_ROOT_ID_GET – this FM is used to fetch the folder ID
  • SO_OBJECT_INSERT – used to insert the converted XML file
  • BINARY_RELATION_CREATE_COMMIT – create relation between attachment and the document (Reference code is attached)

If there is any error in the attachment the file is sent to AL11.

All the received inputs including XML and PDF are saved in a custom table.

Report:

Tcode - ZFI_PAYCOMP_MONITOR

A report is created to display the details received using custom table in which the entries made through ODATA Service and displayed in ALV format.

User can view the error details in description and also can select and reprocess the particular file after updating the missed details.

Created and activated the service ZFI_PAYMENT_COMPLEMENT_002_SRV in following tcode /n/iwfnd/maint_service

The ICF Node should be in active status as shown in the screenshot.

Hope this helps someone! 😊

 

OData NW ABAP Gateway (OData)

entry.title: Create Permission role to have only view access to Employee Central configuration
entry.content:
1. To Make Manage Business configuration as View Only 

In Permission Role, Section: Manage Business Configuration-->All different sections in Manage Business Configuration will be visible as objects and Grant only view access to all sections

Outcome:

No Option to Edit configuration in BCUI

 

2. To Make Configure Object definition as view only

First Secure the object "Object Definition(GOObjectDefinition)" in configure Object Definitions

Now there will be new permission "Object Definition" under Miscellaneous Permission in Permission Role, Grant only view access. Also Grant "Configure Object Definition" access under Metadata Framework

 

Outcome:

No Option to Edit object configuration in Configure Object Definition

 

3. To Make Picklist as view only

Secure the object "Picklist" (Follow same steps as Step 2)

Now there will be new permission "Picklist" under Miscellaneous Permission in Permission Role, Grant only view access. Also  "Configure Object Definition" access under Metadata Framework required to access Picklist Center

Outcome:

Error while trying to edit or create picklist

 

 

4.To Make Business rule as view only

Secure the object "Rule" (Follow same steps as Step 2)

Now there will be new permission "Rule" under Miscellaneous Permission in Permission Role, Grant only view access. Also  "Configure Business Rule" access under Metadata Framework required.

Outcome:

System is not saving the Business rule when you click on "Save".

Step 1: Secure the Object Picklist in Configure Object Definitions.

 

 

Step 2: Unlock the "Picklist" permission under Miscellaneous Permissions in the Permission Role. Grant Insert and Correct access. (Remember to provide "Configure Object Definition" access under Metadata Framework to access Picklist Center)

 

Step 3: While Granting this Permission role, Define the MDF target for the required picklist as below.

Admins accessing the Picklist Center will now see only the defined picklists, allowing edits exclusively to those picklists.

Attempting to create a new picklist will result in an error, ensuring precision in picklist management

entry.title: Stock in Transit quantity discrepancy with PO Quantity while doing Delivery.
entry.content:
Stock in Transit quantity discrepancy with PO Quantity while doing Delivery.

Issue:

When conducting deliveries based on purchase orders (PO), such as stock transfer orders, the PO may consist of multiple line items with varying quantities and different units of measures. Occasionally, quantities, particularly for food products, may involve decimals.

In such instances, the accurate transfer of quantities to transit and the proper updating of the corresponding MSSQ table may encounter challenges due to rounding issues.

Due to these discrepancies, there will be slight variations between the quantity specified in the purchase order (PO) and the stock in transit.

This leads to a quantity discrepancy that prevents users from performing the Post Goods Issue (PGI).

 

The specific error is as follows:

 

The Root Cause of the Issue:

Typically, in such instances, the stock in transit appears accurate, but discrepancies arise in the MSSQ table due to rounding issues stemming from the purchase order (PO).

 

According to the current calculation, 10.140 B17 equals 0.511 MT. However, the MSSQ table reflects only 0.506 MT, leading to an error during the Post Goods Issue (PGI) process, indicating that the quantity exceeds by 0.005 MT.

When attempting to post the differential quantity using 561, it should impact the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) as the material is associated with project stock.

 

Solution:

The resolution involves executing MIGO with movement type 558.

Below fields are the Inputs in MIGO

  • Transaction code: MIGO.
  • Type: Goods Issue.
  • Reference Document: Others.
  • Movement type: 558 with Project Stock – Q
  • Material: - XXXXXXXXXX
  • Quantity: - 0.005
  • WBS: XXXX.XX.XXX.XXXX
  • Batch: 7XXXXXXXXX

 

 

After MIGO:

Following the MIGO process with movement type 558, the stock will be corrected, and the system will permit the Post Goods Issue (PGI).

 

Implementing this solution will have no impact on the actual quantity and budget.

 

Parameters required:

  1. Difference Quantity.
  2. WBS Element.
  3. Movement type 558.

 

Summary:
In instances where stock discrepancies arise between purchase orders (PO) and deliveries, caused by rounding issues, and particularly for Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) based materials, the recommended solution is to use the MIGO transaction with movement type 558 to post the differential quantity.

This approach ensures that the stock is adjusted without affecting the actual quantities or budget.

entry.title: Boosting Business Success with Procurement Analytics: A Journey Paved with Numbers
entry.content:
Grasping Procurement Analytics

In my career, procurement analytics has piqued my interest and kept me engrossed because it combines two essential elements - numbers and procurement. Put simply, procurement analytics is all about utilizing data and numbers to improve buying decisions.

It does this by delving into past expenditure data, understanding the current scenario, and predicting future trends, making our buying decisions smarter and more precise.

I have found procurement analytics to be an effective tool in every decision-making process relating to purchases.

Why Procurement Analytics Matters

Procurement can be a bit like a very complicated puzzle - and to solve it, you need a good grip on numbers and data. That's where procurement analytics comes in handy. It uses numbers to make things more simple and more efficient. I've found, and seen with other successful businesses, that it's a tool you can use to improve processes, boost productivity, and compete better in the global market.

Here are some quite compelling reasons for that:

Real-World Impact of Procurement Analytics

Assisting procurement departments in their decision-making processes, procurement analytics has proven to be a significant game changer in businesses. For example, companies can use it to uncover saving opportunities that may have been previously overlooked.

It doesn't just stop at highlighting saving opportunities; procurement analytics can also greatly improve the way procurement departments interact and work with their suppliers. By using data collected through analytics, procurement teams can have a deeper understanding of supplier performance, helping them ensure compliance with industry standards and regulations more effectively.

This practical application of procurement analytics provides real value to companies and their procurement departments, aiding them in making more informed and effective decisions that improve their bottom line.

If you search a bit on the web, you can generally find that:

Best-in-class companies implemented procurement analytics and observed a 12% reduction in procurement costs within six months, which also led to a 15% increase in turnover. 

Using analytics, they improved poorly-performing suppliers' productivity by 4%, positively impacting overall productivity.

Risk Management with Procurement Analytics

Procurement analytics can be a potent tool for risk management within procurement departments. It gives them the ability to anticipate potential problems well in advance before they spin into major issues. For instance, during a crisis such as the COVID-19 pandemic, procurement analytics could be utilized to foresee potential disruptions in supply chains. This forward-looking approach helps procurement departments plan effectively, ensuring a smoother, more resilient operation despite the challenging circumstances.

If you search a bit on the web, you can find that:

During the COVID-19 pandemic, similar best-in-class companies used procurement analytics to identify potential supply chain disruptions, saving approximately 8% of potential losses.

Boosting Competitiveness with Procurement Analytics

Procurement analytics also plays a key role in enhancing the competitiveness of procurement departments. By harnessing the data provided by this tool, departments can streamline their processes in areas such as product design and engineering. This can lead to significant reductions in costs and improvements in quality, giving them an edge over competitors.

The data-driven insights from procurement analytics can guide a procurement department in making strategic decisions that not only yield immediate improvements but also set the stage for long-term success. This makes it an indispensable tool for procurement departments seeking to optimize their operations and boost their competitive position in the market.

If you search a bit on the web, you can generally find that:

The top-tier companies streamlined operational processes with analytics, reducing operational costs by 11% and improving their product quality metrics by 7%.

With strategic decisions informed by analytics, these improvements led to a quarterly profits increase of 5%, boosting their overall market position.

In Conclusion

From my perspective, using procurement analytics isn't just optional - it's necessary as procurement analytics can help improve various business processes.

I'm curious to hear about your experiences. Have you used data analytics in your business? Do you see how procurement analytics could be a advantage in your operations? Drop your thoughts or questions in the comments below!

And if you found this post helpful, do share it within your business circle. Because when we share knowledge, we all benefit! Let's keep learning together!

Read more about SAP Ariba Spend Analysis and keep an eye on our SAP Road Map in this area.

Regards

Lars

SAP Ariba Spend Analysis

entry.title: Re: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 8
entry.content:
Here are the resources mentioned in this episode:

  • Code Connect event https://code-connect.dev/ 
  • This week's SAP Developer News https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yP27M5hckIY 
  • March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 2 https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/march-developer-challenge-cloudevents-week-2/m-p/13635719 
  • Our working repo https://github.com/qmacro/capb2b
  • (Capire) Common reuse types https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/common#code-types 
  • NPM package that supplies ISO data for CAP common reuse types https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/cds-common-content 
  • ISO content for common CAP types https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/iso-content-for-common-cap-types/ba-p/13637780 / https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2024/03/12/iso-content-for-common-cap-types/ 
  • Service integration with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CodeJam) https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-service-integration-codejam/
  • Milano Centrale railway station https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milano_Centrale_railway_station 
  • (Capire) Views and projections https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/cdl#views-and-projections 
  • (Capire) Configuration profiles https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/cds-env#profiles 
  • (Capire) Hybrid testing (running locally but with a remote persistence layer in e.g. HANA, with the "hybrid" profile) https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/advanced/hybrid-testing 
  • Combine SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud to Create Full-Stack Applications (CodeJam) https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-hana-exercises-codejam
  • Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ("Curiouser and curiouser!") https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland 
  • (Capire) Managed :1 Associations https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/domain-modeling#managed-1-associations
  • Back to basics: Managed associations in CAP (playlist, covering many-to-many associations) https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQCSm1JSRzeBE-BlkygKRAAF 
  • So, You Want to Host a CodeJam! Everything you need to know https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415 
  • OData System Query Option $expand https://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.0/errata03/os/complete/part2-url-conventions/odata-v4.0-errata03-os-part2-url-conventions-complete.html#_Toc453752359

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Supplier Management 2H 2023_Stay Current
entry.content:
Hi @fgaraujo1984 

It will change not right away. 

  1. Please check whether you skipped an assessment to stay current after you passed the SAP Certification exam. If you did, your SAP Certification is no longer valid, and your digital badge will be expired. You can check your completed deltas in the "Learning History" tile on the SAP Learning Hub. In that case you will need to get re-certified via the SAP Certification Hub.

  2. If you did complete the required delta assessment within the respective time period, please check if your email address for taking the assessment to stay current is different than the email address where your SAP Certification is stored (your PT number). In such case, you need to transfer the core certification from your previous user to your new user under which you take the assessment. Information on how to get your certificates transferred can be found here.

  3. If your digital badge is expired, even though you have finished all assessments to stay current and your email address with your SAP Certification is the same as the email address you are using to complete the assessment to stay current and on Credly, please create a ticket in the User Support Center under the category “Digital Badge".

You can find more information on our How to check your SAP Certification stay current status document.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Help editing this query to allow for filtering by OIT...
entry.content:
Hi @alplam ,

Use following Query, You will get Active Button in Filter,

SELECT IG."ItmsGrpNam", IW."ItemCode", IM."ItemName", IW."WhsCode", ISNULL(IW."OnHand",0) AS "OnHand",
'' AS "Batch", '' AS "InDate",IM."validFor" as "Active"
FROM OITW IW
INNER JOIN OITM IM ON IW."ItemCode" = IM."ItemCode" AND IM."InvntItem" = 'Y' AND
IM."ManBtchNum" = 'N' AND IM."validFor" = 'Y'
INNER JOIN OITB IG ON IM."ItmsGrpCod" = IG."ItmsGrpCod"

UNION ALL

SELECT IG."ItmsGrpNam",IW."ItemCode", IM."ItemName", IW."WhsCode", ISNULL(BQ."Quantity",0) AS "OnHand",
BN."DistNumber" AS "Batch", BN."InDate",IM."validFor" as "Active"
FROM OITW IW
INNER JOIN OITM IM ON IW."ItemCode" = IM."ItemCode" AND IM."InvntItem" = 'Y' AND IM."ManBtchNum" = 'Y'
INNER JOIN OITB IG ON IM."ItmsGrpCod" = IG."ItmsGrpCod"
LEFT OUTER JOIN OBTQ BQ ON IW."ItemCode" = BQ."ItemCode" AND BQ."WhsCode" = IW."WhsCode"
LEFT OUTER JOIN OBTN BN ON BQ."MdAbsEntry" = BN."AbsEntry"
WHERE ISNULL(BQ."Quantity",0) <> 0

Thanks,
Prudhvi.

entry.title: IoT ChitChat with Marc Pous
entry.content:
We're thrilled to announce a special edition of the Labs Talk podcast – "IoT ChitChat with Marc Pous," featuring our hosts Sven Haiges & Andreas Neumeier along with guest Marc Pous from Balena. The episode, scheduled for Mar 20 at 15:00 CET, promises a deep dive into the intricate world of the Internet of Things (IoT).


In the upcoming session, our esteemed guests will unravel the current state of connectedness in the IoT realm, shedding light on the latest trends and challenges that define this dynamic landscape. Marc Pous, a true IoT superstar, brings his wealth of experience, having founded thethings.io and currently contributing to Balena's secure container-based technology stack.


The conversation will explore the profound influence of IoT on Customer Experience, delving into how innovative solutions like Balena's empower developers to shape, deploy, manage, and scale fleets of IoT Linux devices securely. The hosts and Marc, having shared the Oktoberfest of Things workshops in Munich, bring a unique blend of technical expertise and a touch of camaraderie to the discussion.


As businesses increasingly pivot towards sustainable practices, the podcast will also touch upon the pivotal role IoT plays in shaping sustainable initiatives. From reducing environmental impact to optimizing operational efficiency, the impact of IoT on sustainability is a crucial aspect of the conversation.


Mark your calendars for this insightful podcast episode that promises to unveil the intricacies of IoT, discuss the current trends, and explore its transformative influence on Customer Experience and Sustainability. Stay tuned for a riveting discussion that goes beyond the surface of technology, providing a comprehensive view of the IoT landscape.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 batch cost used in goods issue
entry.content:
Hi @mark_ownby ,

Use following Query which give Batch Wise Cost for all transactions.

select
(case when T0."DocType"='60' then 'SO' when T0."DocType"='59' then 'SI' when T0."DocType"='162' then 'MR' when T0."DocType"='67' then 'IM' end) as "Type",
T0."DocEntry",T1."BaseNum",T1."DocDate",T0."ItemCode",T1."ItemName",T1."WhsCode",
(case when T0."ActionType"='1' then T0."Quantity" when T0."ActionType"='2' then -T0."Quantity" else 0 end) as "Quantity",T1."BatchNum",

T2."MnfSerial" as "Potency",T2."LotNumber" as "AR NUM",T2."ExpDate" as "Expiry Date",
(case when T0."ActionType"='1' then T0."Quantity"*T0."Cost" when T0."ActionType"='2' then -T0."Quantity"*T0."Cost" else 0 end) as "Value",
MonthName(T1."DocDate") as "Month",YEAR(T1."DocDate") as "Year",T0."MdAbsEntry" as "Line"

from OBVL T0 inner join IBT1_LINK T1 on T0."DocEntry"=T1."BaseEntry" and T0."DocType"=T1."BaseType" and T0."DocLineNum"=T1."BaseLinNum"
and T0."ITLEntry"=T1."LogEntry" and T0."ItemCode"=T1."ItemCode" and T0."DistNumber"=T1."BatchNum"
inner join OBTN T2 on T2."DistNumber"=T1."BatchNum" and T2."ItemCode"=T1."ItemCode"


Thanks,
Prudhvi.

entry.title: SAP Learning Hub 2024 Update FAQs
entry.content:
SAP Learning Hub 2024: Updates & FAQs

Es gibt spannende Neuigkeiten zu SAP Learning Hub, das Interesse an unserem Webinar war groß (über 500 Anmeldungen)! Alle, die nicht dabei sein konnten, finden hier das Recording, sowie im Weiteren eine Zusammenfassung und Antworten auf die Teilnehmerfragen.

Was ist neu im SAP Learning Hub 2024

Der SAP Learning Hub 2024 bietet nun ein verbessertes, optimiertes Lernerlebnis und einen vereinfachten Kauf.

  • Unkomplizierter und kostenoptimierter Kauf: Statt bisher 17 Editionen gibt es nun eine kaufbare Edition für Kunden und eine für zertifizierte Partner.
  • Um möglichst vielen Lernenden einfach Zugang zu SAP-Online-Training zu gewähren, wurden die Kosten optimiert. Weitere Information hierzu finden Sie auf der SAP Learning Hub Webseite - dort können Sie auch direkt online buchen. 
  • 180+ Schulungssysteme mit unbegrenzter Nutzung. Im neuen SAP Learning Hub ist der Zugang jetzt ohne zusätzliche Kosten und ohne Zeitlimit möglich.
  • 4.200+ Live-Sessions in verschiedenen Sprachen und Zeitzonen, die von SAP-Experten durchgeführt werden. Dies wurde kontinuierlich erweitert und ist nun nur noch über SAP Learning Hub zugänglich.
  • Monitoring-Dashboards für Analytics und Lernmanagement für alle Kunden.
  • 4 Zertifizierungsversuche sind im Abonnement enthalten - während die SAP-Zertifizierung eine Transformation hin zu einem stärker rollenbasierten Ansatz durchläuft.

Dies alles ergänzt die bewährten Elemente, wie Lerninhalte in verschiedenen Sprachen, Zusammenarbeit in virtuellen Lernräumen oder die Angebote, aktuell und zertifiziert zu bleiben (Stay Current genannt).

Fragen und Antworten zum neuen SAP Learning Hub

Frage

Antwort

Woran erkenne ich als User, ob ich den alten oder "Neuen" Learning Hub verwende?

Ihre Subskription können Sie einsehen unter https://learning.sap.com/my-learning/subscriptions

Was ist der Unterschied zwischen SAP Learning Hub bisher – also was genau ist neu?

Hier auf Folie 14  stellen wir den Umfang im Vergleich ​
SAP Learning Hub bisher vs. ​SAP Learning Hub neu​ dar.

Wo finden wir Infos, welche Learning Journeys lediglich für die Learning Hub Lizenzinhaber vorbehalten sind und welche Learning Journeys können von Nutzern der Partner Experience durchgeführt werden?

Eine Übersicht der Learning Journeys, die nur Lizenzhaltern zur Verfügung stehen, gibt es leider noch nicht. Melden Sie sich bei Fragen bitte bei tim.kleffmann@sap.com.
Die Partnerexperience ist eine der special Purpose oder Program Editions, wie die Academic oder die SAP Preferred Success Edition – diese können nicht gekauft werden und sind Lernportale für bestimmte Programme.

Weitere Infos für Partner incl. Abgrenzung von SAP Learning Hub, Partner Edition sowie der Partner Experience auf SAP Learning finden Sie auch hier auf SAP Partner Edge.

Ich habe eine Solution Edition, kann ich diese Edition auf die neue Version upgraden?

Leider nein, aber Sie sollten darauf achten, dass die Lizenz nicht automatisch verlängert wird und nach Ablauf der Subskriptionsfrist wechseln.

Wie teuer sind zusätzliche Zertifizierungsversuche über die 4 inklusiven hinaus?

Ein Versuch 200 Euro, 6 Versuche 500 Euro (Kurskürzel CER001 & CER006).

Wo kann ich eine Lizenz für SAP Learning Hub kaufen?

Über diesen Link https://www.sap.com/training-certification/learning-hub.html
Sollten Sie auf technische Probleme stoßen (die es momentan leider noch geben kann), melden Sie sich bitte bei tim.kleffmann@sap.com

Kann man den Learning Hub auch als Privatperson erwerben?

Offiziell über den SAP Store derzeit noch nicht, das ist in Planung für Q3 2024.

Für unsere Lizenzen gibt es ein Auto Renewal. Unsere Lizenzinhaber müssen dann die Zugangscodes neu eingeben. Gehen in diesem Fall die begonnen Lernreisen verloren?

Nein, Lernhistorie wird grundsätzlich automatisch übernommen, falls dem nicht so, ist kann dies über
e-learning@sap.com (Support, mit Email Adresse und Userangaben) beantragt werden, dann wird es manuell übertragen (muss explizit über Support angefragt werden).

Ich habe seit einem halben Jahr eine LH-Professional Lizenz, in der bisher keine Zertifizierung inkludiert war. Ist dies nun für meine Lizenz der Fall?

Leider können Sie erst nach Ablauf der Subskriptionsdauer wechseln und haben dann erst die vier Prüfungsversuche für Zertifizierungen inkludiert.

Gibt es ein Probe Abonnement noch?

Sie meinen vermutlich die bisherige Trial Version bzw. Discovery Edition. Leider gibt es bislang keine für den neuen SAP Learning Hub.

Gibt es die Lerninhalte auch auf Deutsch?

Auf der learning.sap.com-Seite haben Sie kostenfreie Lerninhalte auf Englisch. Mit einer SAP Learning Hub Lizenz haben Sie auch Zugriff auf Lerninhalte in Deutsch (und auch Brasilianisch, Chinesisch, Englisch, Französisch, Japanisch, Koreanisch, Russisch und Spanisch).  

Habe ich das richtig verstanden, eine Learning Journey bereitet mich auf die Zertifizierung vor.

Ja, Learning Journeys zeigen den sinnvollen Aufbau von Trainings für eine Zertifizierung. Alternativ helfen auch die SAP Blended Learning Akademien die verschulter sind auf dem Weg zur Zertifizierung.

Thema Zertifizierung: kann man herausfinden, wie lange eine bestimmtes Zertifikat gültig bleibt, also dessen Dauer?

Wenn Sie es auf Ihre persönliche Zertifizierung beziehen, ist die Information zum Stay Current Programm nützlich.
Wenn Sie auslaufende Zertifizierungen meinen, finden Sie hier eine Übersicht aller Zertifizierungen gegebenenfalls mit dem Vermerk retiring soon. In der Darstellung „Validity“ sind verfügbare und demnächst ablaufende Zertifizierungen nochmal getrennt dargestellt.

Kann man mit der Partner Experience auch das stay current durchführen oder braucht man dafür eine SAP Learning Hub Lizenz?

Eine SAP Learning Hub Subskription ist dafür erforderlich.

Die bisherigen Zertifizierungsvorgaben sind aber weiterhin gültig? 3 Versuche pro Zertifizierung pro Release?

Korrekt. Prüfungsversuche können ab Kauf innerhalb von 12 Monaten absolviert werden. Die Anzahl der Versuche, um eine Prüfung für eine bestimmte Zertifizierung zu bestehen, ist auf drei begrenzt.

Stay Current: Gehören diese auch zu den 4 Zertifizierungsversuchen? oder nur neue Zertifizierungen?

Stay Current Assessments (um eine vorhandene Zertifizierung gültig zu halten oder um sich einfach up-to date zu halten) und die vier Prüfungsversuche für eine Zertifizierung sind zwei verschiedene Dinge.

Beides steht Ihnen über eine SAP Learning Hub Lizenz zur Verfügung.

Ist ein User auf eine Person zugeschnitten oder kann dieser User von mehreren Personen genutzt werden, um dann für z.B. 3 Personen die gleiche Zertifizierung machen zu lassen. Oder benötige ich in diesem Fall 3 User?

Eine SAP Learning Hub Lizenz ist User-bezogen, also nicht übertragbar. Sie benötigen also 3 User.

Wo finde ich zukünftig die bisherigen openSAP-Kurse ?

Die openSAP Courses werden auf die learning.sap.com Seite migriert, damit man alle Lerninhalte an einem Ort finden kann. Die openSAP Plattform wird im Juli dieses Jahres geschlossen – weitere Infos dazu in den FAQ.

Wie kann ich als zuständige Person im Unternehmen für die Lizenzen ein Reporting für alle Lernenden einsehen?

Die "IT Contact Person" erhält automatisch Zugang zum  Learning Analytics Dashboard für alle Lernenden. Beim Kauf können Sie für weitere User eine Admin-Berechtigung beantragen.

Gibt es Beispielfragen zu dieser Prüfung?

Unter diesem Link finden Sie bei "weitere Informationen" zur jeweiligen Zertifizierung Beispielfragen.

Kann ich einen Rabatt als Partner bekommen?

SAP Learning Hub, Partner Edition ist derzeit nicht rabattfähig.  

Wie kann ich Appreciation Points einlösen, zum Beispiel als Referenzkunde?

Sobald Sie eine LH-Bestellung über den SAPStore aufgegeben haben und die Rechnung erhalten hat, bitten sie Ihren Kundenreferenzmanager, diese gegen ihren offenen Saldo zu begleichen.    

Wie kann ich Market Development Funds (MDF) beanspruchen als SAP Partner ?

Wenn sie als Partner berechtigt sind, kann die Erstattung über "Manage My Funds" auf dem PartnerPortal beantragt werden. Dort finden Sie auch weitere Informationen zum Thema MDF.

Wie kann ich mehr SAP Learning Hub Lizenzen kaufen?

Kunden mit Provider-Verträgen können über SAP4Me mehr Lizenzen hinzufügen. Kunden mit 2 und mehr aktiven Learning Hub Provider-Verträgen können aufgrund technischer Einschränkungen nicht nachkaufen und sollten eine Netto-Neu-Bestellung aufgeben.
Die Funktion "Auswählen, auf welchen Vertrag zusätzliche Lizenz hinzugefügt werden soll" befindet sich in der Entwicklung. Kunden mit bestehender VB-Bestellung: Die VB-Bestellung wird umstrukturiert bei Renewal-datum.

Wer hat Anspruch auf SAP Learning Hub, Partner Edition?

Mitglieder des PartnerEdge-Programms sind berechtigt und können über das SAP PartnerEdge Portal kaufen.

Was sind die Zahlungsbedingungen für SAP Learning Hub?

Gemäß den Globalen Geschäftsbedingungen (GTC) sollte der Kunde innerhalb von 30 Tagen für den Abonnementzeitraum im Voraus bezahlen.

Weitere Informationen zum SAP Learning Hub

  • Weitere Information für Partner finden Sie hier im Webinar „SAP Learning Hub für Partner“ am 16. April.  
  • Die Folien des letzten deutschsprachigen Webinars finden Sie hier.
  • Englisch-Sprachige FAQs finden Sie auf der SAP Learning Seite.
  • Wir aktualisieren auch immer die SAP Learning Hub Website. 

Sie haben weitere Fragen? Dann nutzen Sie am besten das Kommentarfeld – wir werden die Liste bzw. Tabelle dann ergänzen.

entry.title: Elevate your performance with the synergy of data, AI, & Strategic planning with SAP Solutions
entry.content:
#SAPDataUnleased2024 announcement solidified SAP’s commitment toward helping organizations unleash the power of their data to make informed decisions, identify and mitigate risk more effectively, harness data to gain a competitive advantage and automatically detect growth opportunities

SAP Datasphere offers a simplified data landscape and through Business data fabric architecture, organizations are able to securely access all their data in one centralized platform regardless of where the data may reside. SAP Datasphere offers Integration with leading data and AI platform such as Collibra, Confluent, Databricks, DataRobot and Google Cloud eliminates data duplication across platforms giving user seamless real time access to data.

Within the centralized Datasphere platform relationships can be build, data can be merged and harmonized and with the new capabilities of SAP Datasphere knowledge graph which enable understanding and representations of real-world use cases of business data organizations can discover hidden insights and patterns that feed into the AI system to drive predictive analytics within SAP Analytics Cloud.

The native integration between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud enables cross-organizational planning breaking down silos and offering a “one-stop shop” for data modeling, planning and analytics. With Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot through open ended and specific questions the tool can automatically generate business plans, data-driven simulations, reports and dashboards.

John “JG” Chirapurath Hero Blog The Future Belongs to Data Driven Organizations outlines how SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud provides a comprehensive strategy for data management, empowering data and planning experts to seamlessly connect, consolidate, and oversee data from various sources in any setting. This is achieved without the need for duplication or intricate processes through the Business data fabric architecture to ultimately enhance the quality of insights gained.

As technology continues to evolve staying at the forefront of data-driven practices will be crucial for organizations aiming to thrive in the future. It’s essential for organization to recognize that successful implementations require a strategic approach, effective data governance and a commitment to ongoing learning and improvements.

entry.title: How to add customizing field for GTT sample extractor code?
entry.content:
A customer or consultant might inquire whether GTT allows adding the customized fields to a specific tracked process and if it can be displayed in the 'Fulfillment Tracking Apps'. The answer to this is affirmative.

Before we begin our task, it is important for you to determine which field should be included in the respective tracked process.

This blog will demonstrate the process of adding the 'Freight Unit Type' field to the 'Freight Unit' tracking process. This primarily involves five steps.

Step 1: Create user model field and add IDoc field mapping for the newly created user model field.

Step 2: Add field mapping in the extractor code in SAP S/4HANA.

Step 3: Add the newly added field to the output structure in SAP S/4HANA.

Step 4: Feed the value for newly added field in SAP S/4HANA.

Step 5: Prepare testing data and check the result in “Check Processes and Events” app or “Track Shipments” App.

 

Step 1: Create user model field and add IDoc field mapping for the newly created user model field.

a.Launch the Manage Models app and go to the GTT standard model “gttft1”.

b.In the Tracked Process tab, choose ‘FreightUnit’, in the right hand under section ‘User Model Fields’ click Create and fill in the field information in the dialogue.

Note: the user model field must start with "ZZ". For example ZZ_TOR_TYPE.

 

 c. After the field ZZ_TOR_TYPE is added, it should appear as follows.

d. Configure the IDOC mapping.

   Click Edit on the top. In the IDOC Integration tab, for Tracked Process field, choose “FreightUnit” from the dropdown list.

Under User Model Fields, fill in IDOC segment and IDOC Field for the newly created user model field.

e.Save you changes and click Deploy on the top right to deploy the model. Once the model is successfully deployed, you can see Active on the model card with its last operation status  “Deployment Success”.

Step 2: Add field mapping in the extractor code in SAP S/4HANA.

To add customized fields to the freight unit, it's necessary for us to find the process class associated with the freight unit. Please do following:

a.In Display IMG page, click Integration with Other SAP Components ->
Interface to Global Track and Trace -> Define Application Interface.

Choose activity Define Used Business Process Types, Appl. Object Types and Event Types. (T-code: /SAPTRX/ASC0AO_CTT - Define AO and Event Types for GTT)

Choose the business process type TMS_TOR from the Define Used Business Process Types on the right side.

Double click Define Application Object Types.

Choose Application Obj. Type GTT_FU and double click.

b.Switch to “Parameter Setup” tab and click the button near the field Cntl Data Function

c.It will navigate to function module ZGTT_STS_OTE_FO_HDR.

d.Inside this function module, you’ll find the process class for freight unit is ZCL_GTT_STS_BO_FU_READER.

e.Double click class ZCL_GTT_STS_BO_FU_READER, then switch to “Attributes” tab and double click the definition for attribute CS_MAPPING, it will navigate to super class ZCL_GTT_STS_BO_TOR_READER.

f.Add field tor_type into the attribute CS_MAPPING, the value should be equal to 'YN_TOR_TYPE', as we have already defined this mapping in the 'gttft1' standard model in the previous step.

tor_type  TYPE /saptrx/paramname VALUE 'YN_TOR_TYPE',

Step 3: Add the newly added field to the output structure in SAP S/4HANA.

a.Open class ZCL_GTT_STS_BO_FU_READER and switch to the “Types” tab.

b.Add field tor_type to types TS_FREIGHT_UNIT.

Please ensure that the field name you have entered matches the ones defined in the CS_MAPPING attribute.

tor_type TYPE /scmtms/tor_type,

Step 4: Feed the value for newly added field in SAP S/4HANA.

a.Double click method GET_DATA_FROM_MAINTAB in class ZCL_GTT_STS_BO_FU_READER and feed the value for field tor_type.

Step 5: Prepare testing data and check the result in “Check Processes and Events” app or “Track Shipments” App.

Once the freight unit is created or critical changes were made, a new IDoc will be generated and sent to GTT.

Example freight unit 4100077180.

a.Check the result in “Check Processes and Events” app.

Launch “Check Processes and Events” app, choose the process type “FreightUnit” and fill in the freight unit number in field “Tracking ID”, press enter. In the detail page, under “User Defined” section, you will see the value of “Freight Unit Type”.

b.Check the result in “Track Shipments” app.

To access the freight unit detail page, we need to navigate from the freight order detail page as there is no separate list page for freight units.

Launch “Track Shipments” app, fill in the freight order number ‘6100004020’ (freight unit 4100077180 assigned with freight order 6100004020) in field “Shipment No.”, press enter.

In the shipment details page, there is a "Freight units" table, through the hyperlink it will navigate to the freight unit detail page.

In the freight unit detail page, under “User Defined” section, you will see the value of “Freight Unit Type”.

 

Summary

By following the above steps customer can easily add customizing field to specific tracked process and shown the fields in the Fulfillment Tracking Apps.

You may use the comments section to provide feedback on this article and help in improving the content. Additional you may post your queries in the comment or in the community Q&A.

entry.title: Integration of a SAP MaxDB into CCMS of an SAP System : Part 1
entry.content:
In this blog provides detailed instructions on integrating various SAP MAXDB databases (Content Server(DMS) and liveCache) into Computing Center Management System (CCMS) of your SAP system.

Why Integrate with CCMS? 

CMS integration offers several benefits:

  1. Solution Manager Monitoring: Include SAP MaxDB and liveCache databases in your Solution Manager monitoring for centralized oversight
  2. Scheduled Administrative Tasks: Utilize the DBA planning calendar to schedule and automate administrative tasks for your SAP MaxDB databases.
  3. DBA Cockpit Access: Gain access to the DBA Cockpit for these databases. This powerful tool offers various functionalities, including:
  4. Database Analyzer: Analyze your database performance and identify potential bottlenecks.
  5. Diagnosis Log Files: Access and review diagnosis log files for troubleshooting purposes.
  6. Database Statistics: View detailed statistics such as table growth and identify the largest tables in your database.

The following Activities Required for Integration (if the system database is not SAP MaxDB).

  1. SAP MaxDB Client Software Installation: Install the SAP MaxDB client software on the SAP application server. This step allows the application server to communicate with the SAP MaxDB database.
    Note: If your system database is already SAP MaxDB, this step is not necessary because the client software is already available on the application servers.
  2. System Environment Extension (Optional): For systems other than SAP MaxDB, extend the system environment to include the SAP MaxDB software paths into the system path variables of the operating system user <sid>adm. This ensures the user has access to the necessary SAP MaxDB tools during the integration process.Note: If your system database is SAP MaxDB, this step is likely already configured correctly.
  3. Registering SAP MaxDB/liveCache Databases in CCMS: Register your SAP MaxDB and/or liveCache databases in the CCMS. This step enables centralized monitoring and management of these databases within your SAP system.

This revision clarifies the purpose of each activity and emphasizes that some steps are only necessary if the system database isn't SAP MaxDB.

The SAP MaxDB client is not installed by default if you are running the DBACOCKPIT on an SAP system that uses a database other than SAP MaxDB. This client is necessary to connect to and manage SAP MaxDB databases from the application servers (Solution manager system).

How to Check for Client Software Installation:

Here's how to check if the SAP MaxDB client is installed on application servers of your system:

  1. Use transaction code SE38 (or SA38 depending on your SAP GUI version).
  2. In the program field, enter RSADA_CHECK_SERVERS.
  3. Execute the program. It will check for the presence of the SAP MaxDB client on all application servers in the system.                                                                                                                        

The program will display results indicating whether the client is installed or not in the server.

      4. Check the check box checking client software and execute.

Maxdb Client Software not installed in the system, So follow the below steps install and configure CCMS.  

Proceed as follows:

  1. Download the SAP MaxDB client software, Client Library and  from the SAP software download center.Follow the path: Databases (Access Downloads) -> Databases and Database Patches -> SAP MaxDB -> Database Patches. Select MaxDB 64-BIT -> MaxDB 7.9 64-BIT and most recent version for your operating system. aslo download SAPCAR XXXX.SAR file for extract Client software and Client library.
    File with name: MAXDBART<XXXX_XX-XXXXXXXX.SAR
    It is sufficient to download the Application Runtime Package.
  2. Extract Application runtime package.
  3. Install it as shown below and run the installation using command ./SDBINST
  4. Set group to "sapsys" and owner to "sidadm" and continue. You can also create standard group and user but make sure that sidadm user has access to read client libraries . example If you are using solman system enter "sapsys" as a group and owner as solman "sidadm" user.
  5. once client software installation completed. Add the environment value of client Software library files location.

    Independent Program Path : /sapdb/programs
    Independent Data Path : /sapdb/programs/bin

    LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/sapdb/clients/MAXDB/lib
    PATH=/sapdb/Programs/bin 

  6. The environment must be adjusted to make sure that the running SAP system gets knowledge about the installed SAP MaxDB client. Adjust environment path variables OS level. 
    Unix/Linux
    - login as operating system user <sid>adm
    - extend settings for the environment variables PATH and LD_LIBRARY_PATH (LIBPATH, SHLIB_PATH) as follows:
    Linux: extend LD_LIBRARY_PATH with /sapdb/clients/<SID>/lib
    SUN Solaris: extend LD_LIBRARY_PATH with /sapdb/clients/<SID>/lib/lib64
    HPUX: extend SHLIB_PATH with /sapdb/clients/<SID>/lib/lib64
    AIX: extend LIBPATH with /sapdb/clients/<SID>/lib/lib64

  7. To change the env move to the directory of "sidadm" user's folder location "/home/sidadm". Inside the folder location find the below files and include the path of the lib and programs/bin for MaxDB client on all application servers. just Logoff and login again from the shell so that the changes can take effect.

    File Name:
    .sapenv_<hostname>.csh
    .sapenv_<hostname>.sh

    .sapenv_<hostname>.csh
    setenv LD_LIBRARY_PATH exisiting lib path add maxdb lib path: /sapdb/clients/MAXDB/lib
    set path = ($path /sapdb/programs/bin)

    .sapenv_<hostname>.sh
    export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=existing lib path:/sapdb/clients/MAXDB/lib
    export PATH=$PATH:/sapdb/programs/bin


    Microsoft Windows
    - Login as operating system user <sid>adm
    - Open Environment Variables setting (enter in search field of Microsoft Windows search panel ENV and select Edit Environment Variables)
    - Change to the user specific section
    - Select variable Path
    - add following paths: <drive>:\sapdb\clients\<SID>\bin;<drive>:\sapdb\clients\<SID>\pgm;<drive>:\sapdb\programs\bin
    - If system wide variable Path contains any SAP MaxDB paths, remove them all.

  8. Download the Maxdb database library ( this is different from Maxdb client libraries) , depending upon your kernel version and Uncar and copy the library dbsdbslib.so to the SAP kernel directory. 
    File with name: lib_dbsl_XXXXX.SAR

    In my case as I am running my sap application on linux environment , so we downloaded the linux compatible library.

  9. Once the environment variables have been set, and the MaxDB library copied to the SAP kernel, for the changes to take effect, restart the application servers.

  10.  Now again check the Client Software status.

     

  11. As of now we are complete the pre requisites of CCMS integration next blog I will complete the remaining steps.
    Check Part to with following URL Integration of a SAP MaxDB into CCMS of an SAP System: Part 2 


    Thanks!
    DINESH S

entry.title: Integration of a SAP MaxDB into CCMS of an SAP System : Part 2
entry.content:
Continuation of Part 1 : Integration of a SAP MaxDB into CCMS of an SAP System : Part 1.

12. After the environment is adjusted you need to re-login as user "<sid>adm" and restart the SAP system.

Registering SAP MaxDB/liveCache databases in the SAP CCMS:

  1. Use transaction DB11 or DB59 to register SAP MaxDB databases.
  2. Create connection using integrate database option to MaxDB database. Enter DB connection name and select database type as MAXDB.
  3. The CCMS integration dialogs are asking for users DBM Operator and Standard Database User. Be aware that the DBM operator for the connection is CONTROL. The database administrator which is used for SQL accesses to the database is SAPR3 on Content Server databases or SAP<SID>. Here, SID refers to the name of the SAP System to be integrated.
  4. Once Maintain the all required details Save the Database Connection details.
  5. Now database connection has been created.
  6. Check the connection test.

  7. Connection established successfully.


  8. Once this has been setup , we are all set to monitor the database in DBACOCKPIT. Using the DBA Cockpit option in transaction DB59.
  9. Now we can able to see the Content Server Maxdb Data , Log, Session, current Settings and db attribute details.

     

  10. Now we can able to schedule the (data/log) backup from here.

     

  11. As usual we can schedule the backup.
  12. Add to create backup schedule for Content Server.
  13. Select complete data backup and select backup template is nothing but backup store file system location and file name with some necessary backup details as per your convivence.
  14. Based on our requirement configure backup recurrence.
  15. Once this complete save it.
  16. If you want to take log backup choose log backup instead of complete data backup.
  17. Now Data and log backup has been scheduled for SAP content Server/DMS.
  18. For more details about CCMS integration refer the below Snote.
    990602 - FAQ: CCMS for SAP MaxDB/liveCache technology 
  19. Bonus information if you are creating template in MAXDB use the following template to take backup Device/File name based on date and time.
    placeholders
    ******************
    #YYYY: year
    #MM: month
    #DD: day
    #hh: hour
    #mm: minute
    #ss: second
    Example
    BACKUP_DATA_#YYYY-#MM-#DD
    BACKUP_DATA_2024-03-14
  20. we cannot use this template #YYYY-#MM-#DD for Log backup.


    Thanks!
    DINESH S

entry.title: Purchase order should not create more than the Purchase Requisition Qty
entry.content:
Dear Team

While create purchase order system should check and validate PO qty  shot not greater than PR qty.

We need BADI to validate while create the purchase order.

Could you provide the BADI to validate the purchase order creation process.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Purchase order should not create more than the Purcha...
entry.content:
There are 2 BADIS that can be used to include your own checks based on your logic code:

 

MM_PUR_S4_PO_CHECK_ALL_ITEMS

This Business Add-In (BAdI) is used in the Purchasing (MM-PUR-PO) component.

You use the BAdI (MM_PUR_S4_PO_CHECK_ALL_ITEMS) to check the entire document.

BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO

This Business Add-In (BAdI) is used in Purchasing (MM-PUR-PO) component and is intended to be used in a Cloud edition only.

You use this BAdI to implement your own checks before a purchase order is saved.

As a modification parameter, you can create messages and display them to the business users. The messages have the following effects:

  • Information messages inform the user but do not prevent the purchase order from being saved.
  • Warning messages alert the user but do not prevent the purchase order from being saved.
  • Error and abort messages alert the user and prevent the purchase order from being saved.

The BAdI interface method is CHECK.

To create translatable texts, go to the tile Custom Business Objects, and choose the tab Custom Code Lists. You can find details about how to implement custom code lists in the comments of the example implementation.

You can find sample implementations of both BADIS in your Custom Logic App. 

Best regards
